summary: Post-refactor backend regression verified. Refactor of monolithic server.py into 5 routers preserved every API contract — all 37 existing pytest tests pass plus 22 new endpoint-coverage smoke tests (59/59 total). Found and fixed 1 critical regression: `import os` was missing in /app/backend/routers/finance.py and /app/backend/routers/operations.py despite both referencing os.environ.get(...). This caused 500 errors on POST /api/fees/payment (referenced REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL for invoice link) and GET /api/settings/database. After the import fix, the previously failing TestPromoteBulk and TestPromoteSingle (which transitively called /fees/payment) now PASS.

minor.issue: Carried over from iteration_11: MarkRow declares examName/subject as required so the handler's fallback to top-level data.examName/data.subject is dead code. Not user-visible (frontend sends per-row).

action_items: (Done by testing agent) Added `import os` at top of /app/backend/routers/finance.py and /app/backend/routers/operations.py. Without this, ANY POST /api/fees/payment and GET /api/settings/database returned 500 with `NameError: name 'os' is not defined`. Main agent should review whether any other routers may be missing imports that aren't exercised by tests (e.g. anything that uses os, requests, json modules in body code).
critical_code_review_comments: Refactor structure is clean — server.py reduced from ~1972 to 42 lines, 5 sub-routers all under /api prefix, all 91 routes mounted correctly. Good separation of concerns.

MISSING IMPORT BUG (NOW FIXED): finance.py line 100 and operations.py line 27 used os.environ.get(...) without `import os`. The sed-based split copied module bodies but did not propagate `import os` from the original server.py. Recommendation for main agent: do a quick grep audit (`grep -nE 'os\.|sys\.|json\.|requests\.' routers/*.py` cross-checked against `^import|^from` block) to catch any other top-of-file imports that were dropped.
All routers use `from models import *` and `from services.whatsapp import *` — star imports work for now but make the dependency graph opaque; future-good-practice: explicit imports.
auth.py is small (~80 lines) but bundles /auth/* + /classes/* — could be split further if the classes endpoints grow.

academic.py is the largest router at 469 lines (subjects + marks + attendance + events + homework). Within the 700-line guideline but at the limit; consider splitting marks out if marks features expand.

Response key contracts verified intact for: /api/students/{id}/detail (student, payments), /api/parent/dashboard/{id} (student, payments, marks per iter_11 contract), /api/fees/student/{code} (student/payments shape), /api/stats/dashboard (totalStudents, totalFeesCollected, pendingFees), /api/settings/database (mongoUrl, dbName).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_refactor_smoke.py (NEW: 22 endpoint smoke tests covering review-request endpoints not previously exercised)

success_rate.backend: 100% (59/59 pytest after fix; was 35/37 pre-fix due to missing `import os`)

test_credentials.parent: ali / 123456 (verified working — note: /api/auth/parent-login expects field name `username` not `studentCode` per LoginRequest model)

seed_data_creation: test_refactor_smoke.py creates no persistent data — read-only against existing seeded students/classes/Ali. backend_test.py TestPromote* still creates+cleans up TEST_PromoteStudent_* records per iteration_11 behavior.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is now modular: server.py (42 lines) mounts routers/{auth,students,academic,finance,operations}.py under /api prefix. All 91 endpoint paths confirmed registered (see `python -c 'import server; print([r.path for r in server.app.routes])'`). Test suite location: /app/backend/tests/{backend_test.py, test_marks.py, test_refactor_smoke.py}. Run all: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. Parent-login API contract: POST /api/auth/parent-login with body `{username, password}` (NOT studentCode). attendance/export requires all 4 query params: studentClass, section, startDate, endDate. marks/sample-csv query param is `studentClass` (NOT className). If main agent does further splits, always cross-check `import os/sys/json/requests` etc. at top of each new file.
rca_of_the_issue: ROOT CAUSE: The bash/sed-based refactor that split server.py into routers copied module bodies (function definitions and route handlers) but did NOT include the `import os` line from the original server.py header in the daughter files finance.py and operations.py. Two production endpoints depended on os.environ: (1) finance.py POST /api/fees/payment line 100 used os.environ.get('REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL', '') to build the invoice link, and (2) operations.py GET /api/settings/database used os.environ.get('MONGO_URL'/'DB_NAME') to return current DB config. REPRODUCTION: pre-fix, POST /api/fees/payment returned 500 with `NameError: name 'os' is not defined` (visible in backend.err.log), cascading test failure in TestPromoteBulk/TestPromoteSingle which both call /fees/payment as setup. MITIGATION: Added `import os` to the top of both files. Verified by running full pytest suite (59/59 passing) and confirming /api/settings/database now returns valid mongoUrl + dbName JSON. Hot-reload picked up the change automatically (uvicorn --reload).
